title: Level 881
wikitext:
{{DISPLAYTITLE:What Grows Below}} {{CSS|content= @import url('https://fonts.cdnfonts.com/css/libre-caslon-text'); html .resizable-container, .page-content h1, .page-content h2, .page-content h3, .page-content h4, .page-content h5, .page-content h6 { font-family: 'Libre Caslon Text', sans-serif; } .theme-fandomdesktop-dark { --theme-page-background-color: #1C1917; --theme-page-background-color--secondary: #292524; --theme-page-text-color: #CAD5E2; --theme-community-header-color: #CAD5E2; --theme-link-color: #FFA2A2; --theme-accent-color: #460809; --theme-accent-label-color: #CAD5E2; --theme-border-color: #CAD5E2; --theme-sticky-nav-background-color: #292524; } .theme-fandomdesktop-light { --theme-page-background-color--rgb: 28, 25, 23 --theme-page-background-color--secondary--rgb: 41, 37, 36 --theme-page-text-color--rgb: 202, 213, 226 --theme-community-header-color--rgb: 202, 213, 226 --theme-link-color--rgb: 255, 162, 162 --theme-accent-color--rgb: 70, 8, 9 --theme-accent-label-color--rgb: 202, 213, 226 --theme-border-color--rgb: 202, 213, 226 --theme-sticky-nav-background-color--rgb: 41, 37, 36 } }}
'''Content Warning'''
This page contains sensitive content that may not be suitable for all audiences. Readers including you might consider these topic(s) disturbing, anxiety inducing, or inappropriate: '''Detailed descriptions of rot and decay and fungal imagery.'''
{{Class 5}} == Description == {{Blurimage|file=TarNation2.png|caption=The first image of the "Black Cacophany" river.|hovertext=Black gold.}} "Level 881, the 882nd level as seen with [[Phenomenon 13|the recurring number]] 880, is one of great misfortune." The words pour out of the professor's mouth hesitantly, as if it were a leaky faucet trying its very best to remain in top shape, as this was the most important announcement of his life up until now. "The entire construct is an underground city, about the size of NYC, and is made primarily out of steel, meaning it's constantly shrouded in an eerie and reverberant darkness, merely penetrated by puny streams of light coming from the daring research bases and individuals that seek out the scraps of what's already been scrapped. Within the reinforced walls of the level, the Godforsaken darkness is punctuated by hundreds of awful skyscrapers and other modern structures, the city appears to have once been full of technological marvels, nearly all of which are now covered in rust from the inconsistently high humidity. Plus, the rot of poisonous tree roots, [https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Dendrochronology dated] to be thousands of years old." The professor flips through his notes, petrified by mere descriptions of a place of which presently exists, despite any attempt to clear one's mind of such, and persistently plagues his mind, for he had seen it through delicate eyes. "Small pockets of the city turning out to be as hot as the inside of an active volcano." As the professor spoke this sentence, he felt the temperatures rise along with the words he spoke as they pinched him on his old, wrinkled face yearning for a solemn closure. The professor stumbles a tad bit, almost blatantly so but nobody could bring themselves to question it, and he spends minutes desperately trying to suppress memories that were surfacing atop viscous tar that should have incinerated them, onset visions of one sheep, two sheep and three sheep occur, lost goners and torched. After a moment of solemn gazes, the professor eventually recollects his spilt words. "There is a lot that goes on outside our vision, as what will happen has already been, for the most part, decided," he exclaimed. "We bury people before they start to decompose, in fear of the unpleasant visions of such, throw milk away as soon as it reaches the expiry date, in fear of the harmful bacteria that have infested the calcic structures; here, however, none of this gets discarded. The next file will tell just how detrimental the culminating waste left behind has truly been, mutating throughout hundreds of years into an amalgamation utterly incomprehensible to the average Joe, unbeknownst to anyone up until very recently." Hesitantly, the professor pulls out a file from a [[:Category:Anti-Entity Group|red folder]] and proceeds to let the entire class read off of it.
== Level 881 - Hazard Dossier == {{Blurimage|file=CreviceShroom.jpg|caption=A sample of the fungus which lives within the crevices.|hovertext=It feels.}}Among the city lay various unfavorable entities, most notably [[Entity 49|Pink Larvae]], [[Entity 124|Clockroaches]], and various Frontrooms pests such as Norway rats (''Rattus norvegicus''), fruit flies (''Drosophila melanogaster''), several species of termite (''Isoptera''), and [https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Manchineel Manchineel tree] (''Hippomane mancinella'') roots. The structures host one noticeable entity in particular, of which is one of the most striking features of the level. A massive white fungus reaches its veins into any seam and crevice it can, releasing hundreds of millions spores in order to moisten the areas it creepily picks with its uncanny, inhuman pseudo-intelligence. Inhaling these spores are a huge threat, as they are known to be able to incubate in the human body and cause major cell damage in a matter of days. Reports of people's corpses being used as an alluring puppet by the fungus have been reported. Its smell is viler than sulfur and soot, and from miles away it can put one right to sleep within the span of an eye blink, shutting them closed forever. Pests fall weak to even this simple yet startlingly intelligent organism, and wanderers are often discouraged from even entering the city. Despite all of the telltale signs of a horrid place, the sheer amount of loot within tempts even the most distant people from all across these Godforsaken [[Level List|rooms]]. Overall humidity levels range from 0.4% to 110%, the latter [https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Supersaturation supersaturation] being caused through the release of billions upon billions of spores, which onsets the slow, weakening, decay of the steel structures to occur as the iron and its alloys of which everything is built upon begin to crumble and become iron oxide, hence rust. Any rust within this level anomalously corrodes things at a much quicker rate than what is typical of rust, requiring only mere hours to visibly consume an entire building. Gamma radiation is constantly emitted by run-down nuclear power plants. [[Object 12|Corrosive red sap]] runs through the foggy streets, damaging the frameworks of many buildings which subsequently cause mass destruction—and causing violent reactions with certain chemicals. One of the most unalluring sights within the confines of the level is what is often nicknamed the "Black Cacophony", a vast river of steaming tar of which hypothetically extends throughout all 300 square miles of the city, with no end in sight having been observed as of yet. Further samples and photographs are required in order to determine the level's geography and ecosystem. == Colonies == Several colonies have repurposed old buildings within the level into fronts for mining operations, including but not limited to; the [[M.E.G.|M.E.G]], [[Auto Nexus]], and the [[:Category:Anti-Entity Group|A.E.G]]. One must be wary of the countless [[Glowfolk]] that venture here, in violent search for resources, and the [[Level 107|Duchy of Cascada]]'s operation known as "Operation Dust Angel". Anyone of whom is reading this file, within this specific inter-group facility, is expected to return with a decent amount of information and/or resources, in exchange for more advanced weaponry. On this mission, each team will be equipped with a small amount of [[Object 60]] and a pistol for each teammate. Always keep in mind, if one encounters an unfamiliar person or group of people, do not trust them, for one does not know of their ulterior motives.
{{Blurimage|file=Fireproxy gabeismail.jpg|caption=}} Gabriel rocks back and forth in his chair, whilst Ismail jots down notes from the file, taking every lecture he had ever attended in his life very seriously. Class was soon dismissed and the two head out, shutting the door as the professor stared at them from the podium, leaving the old man and the eerie quietness in the room behind. The two put on their fire proximity suits, and quickly escape through the large, obsidian-colored pointy gates, out into the strange world awaiting them.
== 1/24/26-881/1 - PoI01207 & PoI01208 Composite Bodycam Transcription ==
Gabriel's dialogue is on the left, Ismail's is on the right, all other text describing the footage will be centered.
Do you think this will really work out for us? The resources listed in the description seem highly unlikely, along with that, who would give out AK-47s for going to a level?
Just be a bit more optimistic for now. There is always something to look forward to within these experiences, even if they're not the cleanliest or the most pleasant.
Right, as if we'll return in one piece.
In all my adventures, I always know that as long as there's a will, there's a way, it just happens in a sort of anomalous fateful way, you know?
I guess.
Back in [[Level 912|that mirage of a dream]], we could have been shanked, but something swept in, a gut feeling made me beg you to leave before the incident occurred and I noticed some like pattern that redirected us out of this series of improbable, would-be pre-determined events. Consider [[Level 799|the invitation]] too, the very moment we were about to get transported to that hellish place, the level just so happened to be reduced to smithereens. Too long, didn't listen, I am confident that we will be fine whenever we are together.
Alright alright, wishful thinking, I suppose, but I... have faith in your abilities. God, I still remember how joyful you were just looking at me.
Love you too, Ismail.
Yep.
L- Let's focus on getting through this mountain of trash, will ya?
Alright...
Hours pass by as the two dig through all the wasted scraps of civilization. A strange feeling somehow, some way manages to fill the vastness of the air. Every molecule—every atom—gives off a strong vibration, never in the past has the present been so clear, uncannily strong for a moment sealed within a meaningless cube. If there was any time to talk, it was now, and Ismail knew that, uncertain of which path he'll go down; the one where they make it out unscathed, basking in the glory of all the accomplishments they have mutually earned, or the path whereas he becomes one mere sentence in the timeline.
Do you see that in the distance? Some sort of... guy, he's moving all weird.
Can't talk, found gold.
Oh uh, really?
Several violent sparks bursting from the aforementioned "gold" begin to claw at Ismail as he exclaims in shock.
OH- shoot, what was that?
Crap, must've been cesium, not gold.
How did you even... whatever-
It was in a tube, alright?
Wait, are you good?
I'm good, and don't even worry about it.
What about that... thing in the distance?
Oh crap... we can't trust him, remember? We should probably head indoors.
Gabriel and Ismail run into what appears to be a nearby rusted "museum" of sorts. In the background, the mysterious man can be seen swiftly thrown off to the side through seemingly unknown means, right against a building, uncannily crunching into chunks in an eerie, unhuman way, all unbeknownst to the two.
Come on now, just go deeper into the building!
The two's footsteps echo throughout and in-between the intricately marbled pillars of the museum, exclaiming to everyone whom beholds the sight of them that everything here has got "all show and no tell". The paintings that litter the sterile walls along the halls are each basic portraits of urban life, no style, and ironically no life. No human hands could have crafted these, no, it was something produced, not crafted. Nothing is said, nothing. The dust in the air contains more clarity than what there is left to show.
Do you see those portraits on the wall?
Huh?
This one, it has a person on it!
Gabriel daintily dusts the glass covering the portrait, revealing the meticulously painted face of a solemn, poker-faced middle-aged man, with hair as black as coal and bronze glasses. Although there was nothing human about the rest of the paintings and contraptions, this one was an exception. There was life behind the man's eyes, real life, as if the soul of the man portrayed inhabited the painting itself.
Huh... who is this guy?
No idea, take it and let's keep moving, time's ticking and we don't have much time left until our demise.
Gabriel delicately and anticipatingly turns the painting around, not let down at all by what he sees.
It has a placard on the back, take a look!
The placard reads "1897 - [name is scratched out], the man of whom had greatly benefitted the industrials of our time, with increased production lines and higher employee wages, it seemed as if we had lived in the waters above the firmament themselves. Unfortunately, after countless scandals involving unsafe work environments, every single factory of his had been shut down, with a stark drop-off in production causing chaos nationwide. Cleanup efforts still persist, but the future is uncertain as of the time of writing."
Strange. Seemed like a real "helpful" guy.
What do you think even happened here? I don't see how all this could possibly have been called heavenly. Hell, I've seen people who've gone to the most beautiful levels that the eyes can perceive act completely neutral any time I try to praise them, so then what would this place be to them?
Hm, well, maybe it did change in quality after this four-eyed guy made these abominations that are tryna choke us to death with these [EXPLETIVE] fumes.
Hey, remember, the bodycams.
Oh yeah? [2 MINUTE LONG EXPLETIVE SESSION, OMITTED FOR BREVITY]
...Jesus, alright then.
Ess-em-aich. ["SMH", SLANG ABBREVIATION FOR "SHAKING MY HEAD", HIGHLY UNPROFESSIONAL, ASSESSMENT REQUIRED]
You did not just say "SMH" out loud.
Yes I did.
The way the cookie crumbles I guess. You seem to be able to do ''anything'' but stay on task.
What about that doohickey over there? The big metal box with gears attached?
Looks like some sort of steampunk generator thingy, it's... ingenious. I did see a watermill outside a while ago, but there's no water left for... ''obvious'' reasons.
The two continue to walk down the museum's dark, looming hallway, briefly illuminated from time to time by shattered, incandescent lightbulbs that glow an eerie yellow, affixed to precarious, yet oddly intricate black ceiling attachments, with specks of orange dabbled on like fiery coal. The two stay very closely-knit to one another, with Ismail turning his head side to side, seemingly holding in some sort of emotion behind that always neutral face of his.
Place gives me the creeps. How long does this thing go on for? If there's a dead end, should we just turn back and go outside? What even is the point of all of this? What exactly are we looking for at this point?
Gabe. I'm here, and I have my gun drawn, a mushroom isn't gonna harm you.
It's a really big mushroom though.
Eh.
In the background, the sound of a bunch of cans toppling over is heard. The two look behind them, with faces of which reflected the professor of whom lectured them.
Gabe... Gabe... what... what was that... what [INAUDIBLE]
I... I don't know, j- just stay back, please.
No, no, we've gotta go.
Please, don't move a muscle.
The two slowly back up, with their sneakers subtly squeaking against the polished checkered floor. The atoms start to vibrate again, the tense feelings rise from the ashes and consumes them in a viscous slurry of fear. Whatever was lurking—couldn't have been human—no—not at all. It feels—it senses. It's here. Ismail takes one backwards step too far and knocks a bookshelf over. Books stridently and rapidly pour out on the cold, unfeeling floor, activating the faulty alarm systems that had remained untouched for a whole century and a few decades. It now knows.
Dammit.
The white fungus appears to spot them, extending its roots as fast as a car on a non-congested freeway. Gabriel and Ismail scatter, unintentionally dropping and leaving some of their essential resources behind. Ismail seemingly shields Gabriel from the fungus, firing his pistol at the horrendous creation of the ignorant. The world felt as if it was crumbling around the two, as the building is actively and ruthlessly smashed to bits and pieces, wrecking both the mechanical, yet also the soulful. It was indiscriminate, feeling no remorse even for the minds and machinations of which brought it into this world. Gabriel cried out loud, prompting it for mercy, but it never gave in again.
It's behind you!
A smaller, twig-like tendril emerges from the mass, grazing Gabriel's fire proximity suit before abruptly being shot off by Ismail.
[PANTING] Dear God, dear God, where... speak of the devil, that door to the left!
The two hastily yet warily scramble out of the building, both prepared to mindlessly blow the guts out of whatever murder monster decides to step out of the mist this time. Distant foliage-like noises can be heard in the background as the two shut the door in an attempt to conceal and move on from their common foe, without ever having to deal with it again.
Good riddanc-
The large white tendril strikes a ground-shattering blow fiercely at the two from atop the museum, knocking Ismail down whilst Gabriel grabs both of their guns mid-air, ready to fire at the unthinking, unfeeling beast.
MY [EXPLETIVE] LEGS!
Hold onto me!
Ismail's legs are seen in a severely crippled state. Gabriel prepares to fire at the tendril, with noticeable hesitation in his movement. A myriad of alarms can be heard in the distance, and Gabriel halts his fire as a dozen bright red firetrucks, each and every one of them painted with a flaming phoenix, pull up and completely surround him, with loud raspy voices all simultaneously commanding him to "Halt!". Another white tendril prepares to strike.
Move!
Gabriel jumps to his left, landing awkwardly on the ground, with the tendril instead striking one of the red firetrucks. Gabriel then proceeds to pick Ismail up, getting behind a firetruck.
Use the guns!
Gabriel grabs both of their guns and rapidly begins to fire at the beast, popping in and out from behind the firetruck in order to remain discreet. Several piercing screams are heard, coming from both the entity and an A.E.G. soldier that had been wounded.
I'm so sorry.
[COUGH] No- Gabe, 'twas me- Me. I convinced you.
It's not time to stop the fight, grab the gun, go.
Got it. Got it.
Gabriel tosses Ismail's gun back to him, and he swiftly catches it with his remaining dexterity before it even hits the ground.
I'm glad I could help for once. [END OF TRANSMISSION]
Exactly two hours later, after several rounds had been fired, the fungal mass retreated into the darkness, having given up after losing 75% of itself to the onslaught of rapid-fired bullets. Once the remaining soldiers received instructions via signal ordering them to look for and retrieve Gabriel and Ismail, they had vanished without a trace or a single footstep in the rubble, inexplicably leaving only their bodycams behind.
== Entrances & Exits == === Entrances === * Certain run-down buildings in [[Level 107]] may lead to another building within this level. * At times, rusted clusters of chains may form in [[Level 108]], which upon interacting with sends one here. * Fire exits on [[Level 464]] may lead here. === Exits === * Graveyards on this level may lead to [[Level 207]] if any instance of a grave is defiled. * Oddly enough, certain structures, of which are painted orange, lead to [[Level 208]], with said structures even resembling the level to a degree. * An underground facility has been reported to lead to [[Level 594.1]]. * There is no true escape in your mind. {{Audio|file=Rusyt_abyss_ambience.mp3|align=center|volume=0.6}} {{Accordion | animation-duration = 250 | accordion-1-title = Open Authorial Information and Media Sources | accordion-1-contents = Seplophobia, the irrational and intense fear and/or hatred of decaying matter. '''Rewrite Author''': [[User:DSInfinite|dodecah]]
